name: Desert Financial
description_short: Arizona-based credit union offering comprehensive banking, lending, and investment services with 50 branches and 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs across the Co-op network.
description_long:
Desert Financial is a not-for-profit credit union headquartered in Arizona that serves members across the state. Founded on principles of sharing success and community investment, the organization has been recognized as the No. 1 credit union in Arizona by Forbes and maintains partnerships with major institutions including Arizona State University. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Desert Financial operates with a member-focused mission centered on improving financial well-being.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, money market accounts, IRAs, and digital banking tools like bill pay and Zelle. In lending, they provide auto loans (new and used), mortgages with first-time homebuyer programs, home equity lines of credit, personal loans, student loan refinancing, and flexible lending options. Their credit card portfolio includes multiple rewards programs (Travel Rewards+, Reserve Rewards+, Everyday Rewards+, Max Cash Preferred, and College Real Rewards). Additionally, Desert Financial offers investment services, insurance products, and a full business banking division with checking, savings, loans, and treasury management.

What distinguishes Desert Financial is its community-oriented structure and demonstrated commitment to giving back. The website explicitly states they returned over $4.7 million to their community in 2025 and emphasizes their not-for-profit status allows them to prioritize members over shareholders. They offer educational workshops, webinars, and financial wellness resources at no cost. The Relationship Rewards program provides benefits for deepening member relationships, and they operate their own auto-shopping service (AutoSMART) to streamline vehicle purchases. Partnership with ASU and community heroes programs further demonstrate their local investment.

Desert Financial is a legitimate, well-established financial institution appropriate for Arizona residents seeking traditional banking with community values. However, as a credit union, membership eligibility may have residency or occupational requirements (not detailed on the homepage). Their rate competitiveness depends on membership tier and relationship depth. While they advertise competitive rates and extensive branch/ATM access, consumers should verify membership requirements and compare rates against national banks for specific products before committing.
services: Free checking with debit cards and overdraft protection
Savings certificates, money market accounts, and IRAs
Early direct deposit and youth checking accounts
Auto loans for new/used vehicles, refinancing, and recreational vehicles
Mortgages with first-time homebuyer and community heroes programs
Home equity lines of credit and mortgage refinancing
Personal loans and student loan refinancing
Multiple rewards credit cards (Travel, Reserve, Everyday, Max Cash Preferred, College)
Bill pay, Zelle money transfer, and digital banking
Digital investing and wealth management services
Business checking, savings, loans, and treasury management
Insurance products including home, auto, life, and Medicare
pros: 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs through Co-Op network provides extensive access beyond 50 Arizona branches
Named No. 1 credit union in Arizona by Forbes, indicating third-party validation of quality
Not-for-profit structure legally required to return excess earnings to members rather than shareholders
Free financial education including workshops, webinars, and financial wellness content
Returned $4.7 million to community in 2025, demonstrating transparent commitment to giving back
Partnership with ASU and specialized programs (Community Heroes, First-Time Homebuyer) show targeted member support
Full-service offerings from checking to investments reduce need for multiple financial institutions
cons: Credit union membership typically restricted by geography, occupation, or family ties—eligibility requirements not clearly stated on homepage
Website does not display specific APR/APY ranges for most products, only sample rates, making comparison difficult
Limited information on overdraft fees, monthly maintenance charges, or other service fees visible on main pages
No mention of CDIC/NCUA insurance limits or member protection details in accessible location
Competitive rates advertised (5.84% auto, 5.804% mortgage) are not clearly better than national alternatives based on website alone
best_for: Arizona residents seeking comprehensive banking with community values and local branch access
ASU students and alumni interested in partnered financial services with school-specific benefits
Credit union members eligible by residency or employment who prioritize financial education and community investment
Consumers wanting to avoid shareholder-driven banking and support not-for-profit financial institutions
diagnosis: Desert Financial is best for Arizona residents and ASU-affiliated individuals seeking traditional, full-service banking from a community-focused credit union with extensive local branch presence and demonstrated commitment to member education and community giving. The main caveat is verifying membership eligibility (credit unions typically have occupational or geographic requirements not clearly detailed on their homepage) and comparing their advertised rates against national competitors for specific products before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
